title: Public Records Request - 30500 Railroad Street West, Ardmore

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records pertaining to Wolverine Tube Inc. located at 30500 Railroad Street in Ardmore, Tennessee (Division of Remediation Site ID numbers 28502 and 28511). The facility was listed on the Voluntary Cleanup Program Inventory (VCP), State Remediation Program List (SRP), and Toxics Release Inventory System (TRIS) databases. The facility may be listed under the name Universal Oil or National Copper. Alternate addresses may include 30498 and 40494 Railroad Street. For the facility's VCP/SRP participation, please include the following record types, if available: initial incident reports, initial site investigations, subsequent site investigations (may be called a "further site investigation" or similar), quarterly groundwater monitoring reports, remedial action plans, remedial action completion reports,  and site closure/no further action (NFA) documentation. As it pertains to the TRIS listing, please include the facility's Tier II reports, Form Rs, and material safety data sheets (MSDS). TRI ID 38449WLVRNRAILR.

In my last communication with the TDEC, they requested that I choose from a list of records (attached) to narrow the scope of my request. The desired records are highlighted in yellow. In lieu of OneDrive, I ask that TDEC personnel send responsive records via email to cdowney@augustmack.com. Records may also be photocopied and sent via postal mail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Caitlin Downey.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFQ# EPSD.DISTRICT.2025.0001

Title/Description: Stop Arm Surveillance for Bus Fleet 

Issuing Entity: East Providence School District

Specifically, I am requesting: 

The Total Number of Buses Installed?

Have their been any citations issued?

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith
